mcp_tool_usage_guide.overview: Specific guidance for using MCP tools effectively during repository audits. This guide covers the content-repository MCP server tools and their optimal usage patterns.

priority.when: Always use first to understand available class types in the repository
priority.why: Provides the foundation for all class-based analysis
priority.usage_pattern: Call once at the start of the audit to get Document, Folder, and other root classes

priority.when: Use to discover all classes of a specific root type
priority.why: Semantic search finds classes better than manual browsing
priority.usage_pattern: Use with broad keywords to discover classes: - For all Document classes: keywords ["document", "class", "all"] - For specific domains: keywords ["HR", "employee"], ["invoice", "financial"]

priority.when: After identifying classes, get complete property information
priority.why: Provides full property metadata including types, cardinality, searchability
priority.usage_pattern: Call for each class you want to analyze in detail

priority.when: To count documents per class or find specific document types
priority.why: Assesses actual usage and validates class relevance
priority.usage_pattern: Use document_search for Document classes, repository_object_search for other types

tool.name: list_root_classes
tool.purpose: Retrieves all root class types available in the repository (Document, Folder, Annotation, etc.)
tool.when_to_use: - At the very start of every audit - To understand the repository's class architecture - Before using determine_class to know which root classes to search
tool.expected_output: List of root classes like: Document, Folder, Annotation, CustomObject, etc.

best_practices.practice: Call this once at the beginning of the audit
Document the root classes in your audit report
Focus primarily on Document and Folder classes for most audits

tool.name: determine_class
tool.purpose: Finds classes matching given keywords by searching class names and descriptions
tool.when_to_use: - To discover all classes of a specific root type - To find classes related to a specific domain (HR, Finance, etc.) - When you need to build a comprehensive class inventory

parameters.parameter: The root class to search within (e.g., "Document", "Folder") Must be a valid root class from list_root_classes
Array of up to 3 keywords to search for in class names and descriptions Use broad terms to find more classes, specific terms to narrow results

strategy.name: comprehensive_discovery
strategy.description: Find all classes of a root type

strategy.name: domain_specific
strategy.description: Find classes in a specific business domain

strategy.name: pattern_based
strategy.description: Find classes with specific naming patterns

best_practices.practice: Start with broad keywords to discover all classes
Use multiple searches with different keyword combinations
Document all discovered classes before analyzing in detail
Look for patterns in class names (prefixes, suffixes)

tool.name: get_class_property_descriptions
tool.purpose: Retrieves ALL properties of a class including system and custom properties
tool.when_to_use: - For detailed class analysis - To understand property types, cardinality, and searchability - To identify custom vs system properties - To document class structure

parameters.parameter: The symbolic name of the class (e.g., "HRDocument", "Invoice")

output_analysis.analyze: Data types (STRING, INTEGER, DATE, BOOLEAN, OBJECT)

best_practices.practice: Categorize properties into system, business, and integration types
Note properties without descriptions
Identify properties that might be duplicates or overlapping
Document unusual data types or cardinality

tool.name: get_searchable_property_descriptions
tool.purpose: Retrieves only the searchable properties of a class
tool.when_to_use: - To understand which properties are indexed for search - To assess search capabilities - To identify properties that should be searchable but aren't

parameters.parameter: The symbolic name of the class

best_practices.practice: Compare searchable vs total properties to assess indexing strategy
Identify business-critical properties that aren't searchable
Note if too many properties are searchable (performance impact)

tool.name: document_search
tool.purpose: Searches for documents based on class and property criteria

prerequisites.prerequisite: Must call determine_class first to get valid class name
Must call get_searchable_property_descriptions to get valid property names

tool.when_to_use: - To count documents in a class (use empty search_properties) - To find documents with specific property values - To sample documents for analysis - To assess property population rates

parameters.parameter: Object containing: - search_class: Class symbolic name - search_properties: Array of filter conditions (can be empty for count)

tool.usage_example_count:
Count all documents in a class
<use_mcp_tool> <server_name>content-repository</server_name> <tool_name>document_search</tool_name> <arguments> { "search_parameters": { "search_class": "HRDocument", "search_properties": [] }, "max_results": 1000 } </arguments> </use_mcp_tool>

best_practices.practice: Use empty search_properties to get document counts
Use max_results to limit large result sets
Sample documents from different classes for comparison
Use CONTAINS operator for flexible text matching

tool.name: repository_object_search
tool.purpose: Searches for repository objects other than documents (Folders, Annotations, etc.)

prerequisites.prerequisite: Must call determine_class first to get valid class name
Must call get_searchable_property_descriptions for property names

tool.when_to_use: - To find folders in the repository - To count objects of non-Document classes - To analyze folder structure and organization

best_practices.practice: Use for Folder class analysis
Identify root folders by searching with empty properties
Document folder naming conventions

tool.name: get_document_properties
tool.purpose: Retrieves properties of a specific document by ID or path
tool.when_to_use: - To examine a specific document's metadata - To verify property population - To sample document quality - After finding documents with document_search

best_practices.practice: Sample documents from different classes
Check property population rates
Verify metadata quality and completeness
Use to validate classification accuracy

tool.name: get_document_text_extract
tool.purpose: Retrieves the text content extracted from a document
tool.when_to_use: - To verify document classification accuracy - To understand document content patterns - To assess whether documents are in the right class - For content-based analysis

best_practices.practice: Use to verify documents are correctly classified
Sample a few documents per class, not all
Check if content matches class purpose
Identify misclassified documents

tool.name: get_document_versions
tool.purpose: Retrieves all versions in a document's version series
tool.when_to_use: - To assess version management practices - To identify version sprawl issues - To understand versioning patterns

best_practices.practice: Sample versioned documents to understand patterns
Identify documents with excessive versions
Document version management practices

tool.name: get_folder_detail
tool.purpose: Retrieves detailed information about a specific folder
tool.when_to_use: - To examine folder properties and metadata - To understand folder organization - After finding folders with repository_object_search

best_practices.practice: Use to understand folder structure
Document folder naming conventions
Identify folder organization patterns

tool.name: get_folder_documents
tool.purpose: Retrieves documents contained in a specific folder
tool.when_to_use: - To see what documents are in a folder - To assess folder organization effectiveness - To understand document filing patterns

best_practices.practice: Sample folders at different hierarchy levels
Check if documents are appropriately organized
Identify empty folders or poor organization

steps.step: 1. Call list_root_classes to get root class types
2. For each root class (especially Document), call determine_class with broad keywords
3. For each discovered class, call get_class_property_descriptions
4. For each class, call document_search with empty properties to count documents
5. Document all findings in the audit report
1. Get class list from previous inventory
2. For each class, call get_class_property_descriptions
3. Categorize properties (system, business, integration)
4. Call get_searchable_property_descriptions to identify indexed properties
5. Sample documents with get_document_properties to check population rates
6. Document property patterns and issues
1. Call repository_object_search for Folder class with empty properties
2. For key folders, call get_folder_detail
3. For sample folders, call get_folder_documents
4. Document folder hierarchy and naming conventions
5. Identify organization patterns and issues
1. Use document_search to find sample documents from each class
2. Call get_document_properties for each sample
3. Call get_document_text_extract to verify classification
4. Check property population and metadata quality
5. Document findings and classification accuracy

error.description: Class name doesn't exist or is misspelled
error.solution: Use determine_class to find the correct class name

error.description: Trying to search on a non-searchable property
error.solution: Use get_searchable_property_descriptions to get valid property names

error.description: Using wrong operator for property type
error.solution: Use = for exact matches, CONTAINS for text searches
Verify class name, property names, and search criteria are correct
